4 Quality Information Document for OMI_CLIMATE_TEMPSAL_BALTIC_Ttz_trend 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-BAL-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE-
TEMPSAL-Ttz-trend  
Issue 4.0 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
OMI_CLIMATE_TEMPSAL_BALTIC_Ttz_trend. 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
This document provides to Copernicus Marine Service users information on the quality 
of the horizontally averaged temperature for the Baltic Sea. The linear trends/slopes/fits 
and their 95% confidence intervals have been calculated using last square 
regression method described in Draper and Smith (1998) eq. 1.2.9 and 1.4.8.  
The daily temperature data is extracted from the BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003_011 
product. The accuracy of the index is directly dependent on the accuracy of the Baltic 
reanalysis and near-real-time products (BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003_011) therefore 
evaluation is based on the QUID documents of the products. 
c) Copernicus Marine products used as input for the computation 
Product name (and Type) Documentation

_011 (model reanalysis)

5 Quality Information Document for OMI_CLIMATE_TEMPSAL_BALTIC_Ttz_trend

CMEMS-BAL-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE-
TEMPSAL-Ttz-trend  
Issue 4.0 – November 2025 
2) SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION  
a) Subsurface temperature trend  
In the CMEMS -BAL-QUID-003-011 the temperature of reanalysis product has been 
compared with all available measurements across the Baltic for period of 1993-2024.  
The bias showed high temporal variability especially at surface where deviations from  
observations were in range of ± 0.2 °C. The average bias remained within range ± 0. 3 
°C at all depths. Largest positive bias was shown in subsurface layer between depths 
from 30 to 80 m.  The average RMSE in that layer was also highest reaching 1.5 °C.  The 
bias was negative in surface layer and deep layer, where mean bias was in range of -
0.25 to -0.07 °C. The RMSE of surface and bottom layer was 0. 9 °C and 0.3 °C, 
respectively.
